200101.english: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are scattered abroad, greeting.
200101.cherokee: ᏥᎻ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎬᏩᏅᏏᏛ, ᏫᏥᏲᎵᎦ ᏔᎳᏚ ᏂᏥᎳᏍᏓᎸ ᎢᏣᏗᎦᎴᏲᏨᎯ ᏥᎩ.
200101.phonetic: Tsi-mi, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-le Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv gv-wa-nv-si-dv, wi-tsi-yo-li-ga ta-la-du ni-tsi-la-s-da-lv i-tsa-di-ga-le-yo-tsv-hi tsi-gi.

200102.english: My brethren, count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations;
200102.cherokee: ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗᏳ ᎢᏥᏰᎸᏎᏍᏗ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎢᏥᎪᎵᏰᏍᎩ ᎢᏣᎵᏩᏛᎡᎮᏍᏗ.
200102.phonetic: I-da-li-nv-tli, u-tsa-ti u-li-he-li-s-di-yu i-tsi-ye-lv-se-s-di, i-yu-no tsu-da-le-nv-dv i-tsi-go-li-ye-s-gi i-tsa-li-wa-dv-e-he-s-di.

200103.english: Knowing this, that the trying of your faith worketh patience.
200103.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎮᏍᏗ, ᎢᏦᎯᏳᏒ ᎠᎪᎵᏰᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏨᏂᏗᏳ ᏂᏨᏁᎲᎢ.
200103.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi i-tsi-ga-ta-he-s-di, i-tso-hi-yu-sv a-go-li-ye-di ge-sv i-tsv-ni-di-yu ni-tsv-ne-hv-i.

200104.english: But let patience have her perfect work, that ye may be perfect and entire, wanting nothing.
200104.cherokee: ᎠᏗᎾ ᎢᏨᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏥᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᏃᏍᏛ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏂᏥᎪᎸᎾ.
200104.phonetic: A-di-na i-tsv-ni-di-yu ge-sv u-ka-li-tsv-hi tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he-s-di; na-s-gi-no ni-hi i-tsi-ka-li-tsv-hi a-le i-tsi-no-s-dv ge-se-s-di, go-hu-s-di ni-tsi-go-lv-na.

200105.english: If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him.
200105.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏤᎲ ᎠᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏂᎬᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏔᏲᏎᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏄᎵᏏᏅᏛᎾ ᏗᏁᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᏓᏍᎦᎬᎾ ᏥᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎠᏎ ᎠᏥᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
200105.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo ni-hi i-tse-hv a-ga-do-v-hi-s-di ge-sv u-ni-gv-ge-s-di, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ta-yo-se-he-s-di, na-s-gi na-ni-v nu-li-si-nv-dv-na di-ne-hi-tsi-gi, a-le ni-da-s-ga-gv-na tsi-gi; a-le a-se a-tsi-ne-di ge-se-s-di.

200106.english: But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.
200106.cherokee: ᎠᏗᎾ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᎯᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏔᏲᎯᎮᏍᏗ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏄᏜᏓᏏᏛᎡᎲᎾ; ᎤᏜᏓᏏᏛᎡᎯᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩᏉ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᏓᎵᏍᏗᎳᏁᎬ ᎤᏃᎴ ᏧᏃᎸᏔᏂᏙᎰ ᎠᎴ ᏧᎨᎯᏙᎰᎢ.
200106.phonetic: A-di-na u-wo-hi-yu-hi-yu ge-se-s-di a-ta-yo-hi-he-s-di, go-hu-s-di nu-dla-si-dv-e-hv-na; U-dla-si-dv-e-hi-ye-no na-s-gi-quo a-me-quo-hi da-li-s-di-la-ne-gv u-no-le tsu-no-lv-ta-ni-do-ho a-le tsu-ge-hi-do-ho-i.

200107.english: For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord.
200107.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏛᎩᏁᎵ, ᏰᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
200107.phonetic: Tle-s-di na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya, U-gv-wi-yu-hi go-hu-s-di dv-gi-ne-li, ye-li-s-ge-s-di.

200108.english: A double minded man is unstable in all his ways.
200108.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏔᎵ ᎢᏳᏓᎢ ᎤᏓᏅᏛᎢ ᎤᏁᏟᏴᏌᏘ ᎨᏐᎢ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ.
200108.phonetic: A-s-ga-ya ta-li i-yu-da-i u-da-nv-dv-i u-ne-tli-yv-sa-ti ge-so-i ni-ga-v du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i.

200109.english: Let the brother of low degree rejoice in that he is exalted:
200109.cherokee: ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏟ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᏩᎵᎮᎵᎩ ᎠᏥᏌᎳᏓᏅᎢ,
200109.phonetic: I-tsa-da-nv-tli ge-sv u-yo i-yu-dv-hna-de-gi wa-li-he-li-gi a-tsi-sa-la-da-nv-i,

200110.english: But the rich, in that he is made low: because as the flower of the grass he shall pass away.
200110.cherokee: ᎤᏪᎿᎭᎢᏍᎩᏂ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎾᎬᏁᎸᎢ; ᎧᏃᏍᎦᏰᏃ ᎤᏥᎸᏒ ᏥᎦᎶᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏓᎦᎶᏐᏂ.
200110.phonetic: U-we-hna-i-s-gi-ni e-la-di na-gv-ne-lv-i; ka-no-s-ga-ye-no u-tsi-lv-sv tsi-ga-lo-s-go na-s-gi-ya da-ga-lo-so-ni.

200111.english: For the sun is no sooner risen with a burning heat, but it withereth the grass, and the flower thereof falleth, and the grace of the fashion of it perisheth: so also shall the rich man fade away in his ways.
200111.cherokee: ᎵᎧᎸᎩᏉᏰᏃ ᏅᏙ ᎤᏗᎴᎩ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎬᏴᏗᏍᎪ ᎧᏃᏍᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏥᎸᏒ ᎦᏙᎠᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏬᏚᎯᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏙᏢᏒ ᎠᏲᎪᎢ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏪᎿᎭᎢ ᏓᎬᏴᏏ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ.
200111.phonetic: Li-ka-lv-gi-quo-ye-no nv-do u-di-le-gi, hna-quo gv-yv-di-s-go ka-no-s-ga, a-le u-tsi-lv-sv ga-do-a-s-go-i, a-le u-wo-du-hi-yu ge-sv u-do-tlv-sv a-yo-go-i.  Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo u-we-hna-i da-gv-yv-si du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i.

200112.english: Blessed is the man that endureth temptation: for when he is tried, he shall receive the crown of life, which the Lord hath promised to them that love him.
200112.cherokee: ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᎪᎵᏰᏍᎩ ᏧᏱᎵᏙᎰ ᎬᏂᏗᏳ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎠᏥᎪᎵᏰᎥᎯᏰᏃ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎠᎵᏍᏚᎶ ᎠᏥᏅᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏧᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎬᏩᎨᏳᎯ.
200112.phonetic: O-si-yu i-yu-li-s-da-ne-di na-s-gi na a-s-ga-ya u-go-li-ye-s-gi tsu-yi-li-do-ho gv-ni-di-yu tsi-ge-so-i, a-tsi-go-li-ye-v-hi-ye-no ge-se-s-di gv-ni-dv a-li-s-du-lo a-tsi-nv-ne-di ge-se-s-di, na-s-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi tsu-du-i-s-da-ne-lv-hi tsi-gi gv-wa-ge-yu-hi.

200113.english: Let no man say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God: for God cannot be tempted with evil, neither tempteth he any man:
200113.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏥᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎩᎪᎵᏰᎭ ᎤᏛᏅᎩ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏰᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎦᏰᏥᎪᎵᏰᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎤᏲ ᎠᎬᏓᏁᎲᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎩᎶ ᏯᎪᎵᏰᏍᎪᎢ.
200113.phonetic: Tle-s-di gi-lo i-yu-no a-tsi-go-li-ye-s-ge-s-di, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-gi-go-li-ye-ha u-dv-nv-gi; U-ne-la-nv-hi-ye-no v-tla ga-ye-tsi-go-li-ye-di yi-gi u-yo a-gv-da-ne-hv-i, v-tla a-le u-wa-sv gi-lo ya-go-li-ye-s-go-i.

200114.english: But every man is tempted, when he is drawn away of his own lust, and enticed.
200114.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏥᎪᎵᏰᏍᎪᎢ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᏧᏘᏁᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏌᏛᎥᏍᎪᎢ.
200114.phonetic: Gi-lo-s-gi-ni a-tsi-go-li-ye-s-go-i i-yu-no u-wa-sv u-du-li-s-gv tsu-ti-ne-hi a-le tsu-sa-dv-v-s-go-i.

200115.english: Then when lust hath conceived, it bringeth forth sin: and sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death.
200115.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏚᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏙᏢᎾ ᎠᏍᎦᏂ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ; ᎠᏍᎦᏂᏃ ᎠᏍᏆᏓ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪᎢ.
200115.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-du-lv-di ge-sv a-do-tlv-na a-s-ga-ni ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go-i; a-s-ga-ni-no a-s-qua-da hna-quo a-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go-i.

200116.english: Do not err, my beloved brethren.

200117.english: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, and cometh down from the Father of lights, with whom is no variableness, neither shadow of turning.
200117.cherokee: ᏂᎦᎥ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎣᏌᏂ ᎠᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏠᎠᏏᎯᎭ ᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᏍᏛᏱ ᎢᎦᎦᏘ ᎤᏪᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏓᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎦᏔᎲᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏄᏓᏴᎳᏛᏫ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200117.phonetic: Ni-ga-v o-s-dv a-da-ne-di ge-sv-i, a-le ni-ga-v o-sa-ni a-da-ne-di ge-sv ga-lv-la-di di-da-le-hv-s-ga a-le a-tlo-a-si-hi-ha di-da-le-hv-s-ga A-ga-yv-li-ge-s-dv-yi i-ga-ga-ti u-we-hi, na-s-gi gv-wa-da-ne-tli-yv-s-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, a-le gv-wa-ga-ta-hv-s-di ge-sv nu-da-yv-la-dv-wi ge-sv-i.

200118.english: Of his own will begat he us with the word of truth, that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures.
200118.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᏛ ᎢᎩᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒ ᎤᏩᏔᏅ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎦᏰᎪᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎡᎦᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᎦᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200118.phonetic: U-wa-sv u-da-nv-te-dv i-gi-na-nu-go-wi-sv u-wa-ta-nv ka-no-he-dv ga-ye-go-gi ni-ge-sv-na, na-s-gi a-yv i-gv-yi e-ga-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi-ya i-ga-li-s-do-di-yi na-s-gi ge-ga-ne-la-nv-hi ge-sv-i.

200119.english: Wherefore, my beloved brethren, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath:
200119.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᎨᏳᎢ ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᎾᏂᎥ ᎣᏏᏄᎵᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ Ꮎ ᎤᏛᎪᏗᏱ, ᎤᏍᎦᏃᎵᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏁᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎤᏍᎦᏃᎵᏳ ᎤᏂᏔᎳᏬᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
200119.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsv-ge-yu-i i-da-li-nv-tli, na-ni-v o-si-nu-li-yu ge-se-s-di na u-dv-go-di-yi, u-s-ga-no-li-yu ge-se-s-di u-ni-ne-i-s-di-yi, u-s-ga-no-li-yu u-ni-ta-la-wo-hi-s-di-yi.

200120.english: For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.
200120.cherokee: ᏴᏫᏰᏃ ᎤᏔᎳᏬᏍᎬᎢ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎪ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
200120.phonetic: Yv-wi-ye-no u-ta-la-wo-s-gv-i v-tla yi-ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-go du-yu-go-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga.

200121.english: Wherefore lay apart all filthiness and superfluity of naughtiness, and receive with meekness the engrafted word, which is able to save your souls.
200121.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏂᏨᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎶᏒᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏲ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᏔᏅᎭ ᏕᏣᏓᏂᎸᏨᎭ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎬᏱᏝᏅᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏗᎬᏩᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᏥᎩ ᏗᏣᏓᏅᏙᎩ.
200121.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-yv-dv ni-tsv-ne-he-s-di ni-ga-v ga-da-ha ge-sv-i a-le u-lo-sv-s-ta-nv-hi u-yo ge-sv-i, a-le u-da-do-li-s-di i-tsv-ta-nv-ha de-tsa-da-ni-lv-tsv-ha ka-no-he-dv gv-yi-tla-nv-hi, na-s-gi ye-li-quo di-gv-wa-s-de-lv-di tsi-gi di-tsa-da-nv-do-gi.

200122.english: But be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.
200122.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏞᏍᏗᏃ ᎢᏣᏛᎩᏍᎩᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎢᏨᏒ ᏱᏣᏓᎵᏓᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
200122.phonetic: A-se-no ka-no-he-dv ni-ga-we-s-gv i-tsa-dv-ne-hi ge-se-s-di, tle-s-di-no i-tsa-dv-gi-s-gi-quo u-wa-sv yi-ge-se-s-di, i-tsv-sv yi-tsa-da-li-da-s-di-s-ge-s-di.

200123.english: For if any be a hearer of the word, and not a doer, he is like unto a man beholding his natural face in a glass:
200123.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏛᎩᏍᎩᏉ ᏱᎩ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᎯᏃ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏓᏤᎸ ᏣᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᎧᏛᎢ ᎠᏓᎨᏗᏱ;
200123.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no gi-lo a-dv-gi-s-gi-quo yi-gi i-ya-dv-ne-hi-no ni-ge-sv-na yi-gi ka-no-he-dv ni-ga-we-s-gv-i, na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya da-tse-lv tsa-go-wa-ti-s-go nu-s-dv u-wa-sv u-ka-dv-i a-da-ge-di-yi;

200124.english: For he beholdeth himself, and goeth his way, and straightway forgetteth what manner of man he was.
200124.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏒᏰᏃ ᎠᏓᎪᏩᏘᏍᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᏅᏍᎪᎢ, ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᏩᎨᏫᏍᎪ ᏄᏍᏛᎢ.
200124.phonetic: U-wa-sv-ye-no a-da-go-wa-ti-s-go-i, a-le a-da-nv-s-go-i, gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv u-wa-ge-wi-s-go nu-s-dv-i.

200125.english: But whoso looketh into the perfect law of liberty, and continueth therein, he being not a forgetful hearer, but a doer of the work, this man shall be blessed in his deed.
200125.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᎪᎵᏰᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎧᎵᎸᎯ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎬᏓᎧᎲᏍᎩ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᏯᎢᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎤᏩᎨᏫᏌᏘ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏛᎩᏍᎩ, ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200125.phonetic: Gi-lo-s-gi-ni o-s-dv a-go-li-ye-s-ge-s-di a-ka-li-lv-hi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di gv-da-ka-hv-s-gi tsi-gi, a-le ga-ya-i-se-s-di, na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya o-s-dv u-da-nv-da-di-s-di ge-se-s-di du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i, na-s-gi-ye-no u-wa-ge-wi-sa-ti ni-ge-sv-na a-dv-gi-s-gi, tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi-s-gi-ni di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i.

200126.english: If any man among you seem to be religious, and bridleth not his tongue, but deceiveth his own heart, this man's religion is vain.
200126.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏤᎲ ᏳᏓᏑᏯ ᏧᏁᎶᏗ ᏱᏅᏩᏍᏗ, ᏂᎦᎾᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎾᏃ ᏱᎩ ᎦᏃᎪᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎾᏫ ᎦᎵᏓᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᏱᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏧᏁᎶᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏎᏉᎢ.
200126.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo i-tse-hv yu-da-su-ya tsu-ne-lo-di yi-nv-wa-s-di, ni-ga-na-hi-s-di-s-gv-na-no yi-gi ga-no-go-i, u-wa-sv-quo-s-gi-ni u-na-wi ga-li-da-s-di-s-gi yi-gi, na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya tsu-ne-lo-di ge-sv a-se-quo-i.

200127.english: Pure religion and undefiled before God and the Father is this, To visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, and to keep himself unspotted from the world.
200127.cherokee: ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᏁᎶᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᎦᏔᎲ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏗ, ᏗᏩᏛᎯᏓᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᎾᏓᏂᏯᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏃᏑᎶᏨᎯ ᎤᏲ ᏄᎾᏛᎾᏕᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏄᏚᏯᏍᏛᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏄᎾᏍᏛ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
200127.phonetic: O-s-dv di-ne-lo-do-di ge-sv a-le ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na a-ga-ta-hv U-ne-la-nv-hi A-ga-yv-li-ge-i, hi-a nu-s-di, Di-wa-dv-hi-da-s-di-yi tsu-na-da-ni-ya-dv a-le tsu-no-su-lo-tsv-hi u-yo nu-na-dv-na-de-gv-i, a-le u-li-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi u-wa-sv nu-du-ya-s-dv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi e-lo-hi a-ne-hi nu-na-s-dv i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

200201.english: My brethren, have not the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Lord of glory, with respect of persons.
200201.cherokee: ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᎡᏦᏳᏒ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏔᎵ ᏳᏛᏕᏍᏗ ᏗᏓᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200201.phonetic: I-da-li-nv-tli, e-tso-yu-sv Ga-lv-quo-di-yu U-gv-wi-yu-hi i-ga-tse-li Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv, tle-s-di ta-li yu-dv-de-s-di di-da-lv-quo-do-di ge-sv-i.

200202.english: For if there come unto your assembly a man with a gold ring, in goodly apparel, and there come in also a poor man in vile raiment;
200202.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏰᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏳᏴᎵᎸ ᏕᏥᎳᏫᎥᎢ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏓᎶᏂᎨ ᎤᎵᏰᏑᏍᏙᎩ ᏧᏬᏚᎯ ᏧᏄᏩᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉᏃ ᏳᏴᎵᎸ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᏛᎾᏕᎩ ᏗᎦᏓᎭ ᏧᏄᏩᎢ;
200202.phonetic: I-yu-ye-no gi-lo a-s-ga-ya yu-yv-li-lv de-tsi-la-wi-v-i a-de-lv da-lo-ni-ge u-li-ye-su-s-do-gi tsu-wo-du-hi tsu-nu-wa-i, na-s-quo-no yu-yv-li-lv u-yo i-yu-dv-na-de-gi di-ga-da-ha tsu-nu-wa-i;

200203.english: And ye have respect to him that weareth the gay clothing, and say unto him, Sit thou here in a good place; and say to the poor, Stand thou there, or sit here under my footstool:
200203.cherokee: ᏰᏥᎸᏉᏔᏅᏃ ᏧᏬᏚᎯ ᏧᏄᏩᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏱᏁᏥᏪᏎᎸ, ᎠᏂ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎯᎲᎦ, ᎤᏲᏃ ᎢᏳᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏱᏁᏥᏪᏎᎸ, ᎾᎿᎭᎾ ᎯᏙᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂ ᎯᎲᎦ ᎯᏆᎳᏏᏗᏱ ᎠᎩᏍᎩᎸ ᎭᏫᏂᏗᏢ;
200203.phonetic: Ye-tsi-lv-quo-ta-nv-no tsu-wo-du-hi tsu-nu-wa-i, a-le hi-a yi-ne-tsi-we-se-lv, A-ni o-s-dv ge-sv hi-hv-ga, u-yo-no i-yu-dv-hna-de-gi hi-a yi-ne-tsi-we-se-lv, Na-hna na hi-do-ge-s-di, a-le a-ni hi-hv-ga hi-qua-la-si-di-yi a-gi-s-gi-lv ha-wi-ni-di-tlv;

200204.english: Are ye not then partial in yourselves, and are become judges of evil thoughts?
200204.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏕᏣᏓᏅᏛ ᏱᏗᏣᏓᎸᏉᏗᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏝᏍᎪ ᏗᏧᎪᏗᏍᎩ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏖᏍᎩ ᏱᏂᏣᎵᏍᏔᏅ?
200204.phonetic: Tle-s-di hna-quo de-tsa-da-nv-dv yi-di-tsa-da-lv-quo-di-ha, a-le tla-s-go di-tsu-go-di-s-gi u-yo i-tsa-da-nv-te-s-gi yi-ni-tsa-li-s-ta-nv?

200205.english: Hearken, my beloved brethren, Hath not God chosen the poor of this world rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom which he hath promised to them that love him?
200205.cherokee: ᎢᏣᏛᎬᎦ ᎢᏨᎨᏳᎢ ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏱᏚᏑᏰᏒ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎤᏁᎿᎭᎢᏳ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏙᎵᎦ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎬᏩᎨᏳᎯ?
200205.phonetic: I-tsa-dv-gv-ga i-tsv-ge-yu-i i-da-li-nv-tli, Tla-s-go U-ne-la-nv-hi yi-du-su-ye-sv u-yo i-yu-na-dv-hna-de-gi e-lo-hi a-ne-hi, u-ne-hna-i-yu go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le u-na-do-li-ga u-tse-li-go-hi na-s-gi tsu-du-i-s-da-ne-lv-hi tsi-gi gv-wa-ge-yu-hi?

200206.english: But ye have despised the poor. Do not rich men oppress you, and draw you before the judgment seats?
200206.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᏰᏥᎦᏌᏯᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᏛᎿᎭᏕᎩ.  ᏝᏍᎪ ᏧᏁᎿᎭᎢ ᎤᏲ ᏱᏂᎨᏨᎿᎭᏕᎪᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏱᏗᎨᏣᏘᏃᎯᎰ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ?
200206.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni v-tla ye-tsi-ga-sa-ya-s-ta-nv u-yo i-yu-dv-hna-de-gi.  Tla-s-go tsu-ne-hna-i u-yo yi-ni-ge-tsv-hna-de-go-i, a-le yi-di-ge-tsa-ti-no-hi-ho di-gu-go-do-di-yi?

200207.english: Do not they blaspheme that worthy name by the which ye are called?
200207.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᏯᏂᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎯ ᏤᏦᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ?
200207.phonetic: Tla-s-go ya-ni-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-go ga-lv-quo-di-yu de-u-do-v na-s-gi ni-hi tse-tso-s-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi?

200208.english: If ye fulfill the royal law according to the scripture, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself, ye do well:
200208.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎢᏥᎧᎵᎢᎮᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᎬᏅ ᎪᏪᎸᎢ, "ᏨᏒ ᏥᏂᏣᏓᎨᏳᎭ ᏂᎨᏳᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᎥ ᎢᏗᏍᏓᏓᎳ," ᏣᏗᎭ, ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏂᏣᏛᎦ.
200208.phonetic: I-yu-no i-tsi-ka-li-i-he-s-di ga-lv-quo-di-yu di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di na-s-gi-ya ni-gv-nv go-we-lv-i, "Tsv-sv tsi-ni-tsa-da-ge-yu-ha ni-ge-yu-se-s-di na-v i-di-s-da-da-la," tsa-di-ha, o-si-yu yi-ni-tsa-dv-ga.

200209.english: But if ye have respect to persons, ye commit sin, and are convinced of the law as transgressors.
200209.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃ ᏙᏣᏓᎸᏉᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗᏉ, ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏅᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎾᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏕᏧᎪᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200209.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni-no do-tsa-da-lv-quo-di-s-ge-s-di-quo, i-tsi-s-ga-nv-ge-s-di, a-le di-ka-na-wa-dv-s-di de-tsu-go-da-ne-he-s-di i-tsi-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi ge-sv-i.

200210.english: For whosoever shall keep the whole law, and yet offend in one point, he is guilty of all.
200210.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏱᏓᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎦ, ᏑᏓᎴᎩᏉᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏯᏲᏍᏗᎭ, ᏂᎦᏛ ᏚᏲᏍᏔᏃᎢ.
200210.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no ni-ga-dv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di yi-da-ka-hna-wa-de-ga, su-da-le-gi-quo-no go-hu-s-di ya-yo-s-di-ha, ni-ga-dv du-yo-s-ta-no-i.

200211.english: For he that said, Do not commit adultery, said also, Do not kill. Now if thou commit no adultery, yet if thou kill, thou art become a transgressor of the law.
200211.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏪᏛ ᏥᎩ, "ᏞᏍᏗ ᏣᏓᏲᏁᎸᎩ," ᎾᏍᏉ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏳᏪᏛ ᎢᎩ, "ᏞᏍᏗ ᏣᏓᎸᎩ;" ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏃ ᏂᏣᏓᏲᏁᎸᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᏱᏣᏓᎸ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎯᏲᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ.
200211.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no i-yu-we-dv tsi-gi, "Tle-s-di tsa-da-yo-ne-lv-gi," na-s-quo hi-a i-yu-we-dv i-gi, "Tle-s-di tsa-da-lv-gi;" na-s-gi-no i-yu-no ni-tsa-da-yo-ne-lv-na yi-gi, a-se-no yi-tsa-da-lv, Hna-quo di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di hi-yo-s-di-s-gi nu-li-s-ta-nv.

200212.english: So speak ye, and so do, as they that shall be judged by the law of liberty.
200212.cherokee: ᎢᏥᏁᎬᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᏁᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏗᎨᎫᎪᏓᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎬᏓᎧᎲᏍᎩ ᎨᎬᏓᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ.
200212.phonetic: I-tsi-ne-gv-i a-le de-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i na-s-gi-ya ge-se-s-di nu-s-dv u-ni-ne-i-s-di ge-sv a-le tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv na-s-gi na di-ge-gu-go-da-ne-di tsi-gi di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di gv-da-ka-hv-s-gi ge-gv-da-ne-di tsi-gi.

200213.english: For he shall have judgment without mercy, that hath shewed no mercy; and mercy rejoiceth against judgment.
200213.cherokee: ᎾᏥᏙᎵᎬᎾᏰᏃ ᏣᎫᎪᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏄᏓᏙᎵᏨᎯ; ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏃ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏓᎵᏁᏗᏕᎭ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200213.phonetic: Na-tsi-do-li-gv-na-ye-no tsa-gu-go-da-ne-di ge-se-s-di nu-da-do-li-tsv-hi; a-da-do-li-s-di-no ge-sv a-da-li-ne-di-de-ha di-gu-go-do-di ge-sv-i.

200214.english: What doth it profit, my brethren, though a man say he hath faith, and have not works? can faith save him?
200214.cherokee: ᎦᏙ ᎬᏙᏗ, ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎠᏉᎯᏳᎭ ᏯᏗᎭ, ᏂᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎾᏃ ᏱᎩ?  ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏒᏍᎪ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏳᏍᏕᎸ?
200214.phonetic: Ga-do gv-do-di, i-da-li-nv-tli, i-yu-no gi-lo a-quo-hi-yu-ha ya-di-ha, ni-du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-na-no yi-gi?  U-wo-hi-yu-sv-s-go ye-li-quo yu-s-de-lv?

200215.english: If a brother or sister be naked, and destitute of daily food,
200215.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎢᎩᏅᏟ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎩᏙ ᏧᏂᏰᎸᎭ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏳᏂᏂᎬᎦ ᏂᏚᎩᏨᏂᏒ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ,
200215.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo i-gi-nv-tli a-le i-gi-do tsu-ni-ye-lv-ha yi-gi, a-le yu-ni-ni-gv-ga ni-du-gi-tsv-ni-sv a-li-s-da-yv-di ge-sv-i,

200216.english: And one of you say unto them, Depart in peace, be ye warmed and filled; notwithstanding ye give them not those things which are needful to the body; what doth it profit?
200216.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏣᏓᏡᎬ ᎯᎠ ᏱᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸ; ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏥᏤᎾ, ᎢᏥᎦᎾᏬᏒᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏦᎸᏒᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᏂᏗᏥᏁᎲᎾᏃ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏰᎸ ᎤᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᎬᏙᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ?
200216.phonetic: Gi-lo-no ni-hi i-tsa-da-tlu-gv hi-a yi-ni-du-we-se-lv; Nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv tsi-tse-na, i-tsi-ga-na-wo-sv-hi a-le di-tso-lv-sv-hi ge-se-s-di; ni-di-tsi-ne-hv-na-no i-ge-se-s-di a-ye-lv u-li-s-de-lv-do-di ge-sv-i; ga-do gv-do-di na-s-gi?

200217.english: Even so faith, if it hath not works, is dead, being alone.
200217.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏔᎵ ᏄᏛᏛᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᎵᏬᏨᎯ, ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
200217.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, i-yu-no ta-li nu-dv-dv-na i-ge-se-s-di di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i, u-li-wo-tsv-hi, u-wa-sv-quo ge-sv i-yu-s-di.

200218.english: Yea, a man may say, Thou hast faith, and I have works: shew me thy faith without thy works, and I will shew thee my faith by my works.
200218.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎩᎶ ᎯᎠ ᏱᏅᎦᏫ, ᏂᎯ ᏦᎯᏳᎭ, ᎠᏴᏃ ᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎭ; ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏍᏋᏂᏏ ᏦᎯᏳᏒ ᏕᏣᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᏅᏗᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏴᏃ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏅᏓᎬᏴᏁᎵ ᎠᏉᎯᏳᏒ ᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎬᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
200218.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi gi-lo hi-a yi-nv-ga-wi, Ni-hi tso-hi-yu-ha, a-yv-no da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-ha; gv-ni-ge-sv ni-s-quv-ni-si tso-hi-yu-sv de-tsa-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv nv-di-s-gv-na ge-sv-i, a-yv-no gv-ni-ge-sv nv-da-gv-yv-ne-li a-quo-hi-yu-sv da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv gv-di-s-gv-i.

200219.english: Thou believest that there is one God; thou doest well: the devils also believe, and tremble.
200219.cherokee: ᏂᎯ ᎰᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎡᎲ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.  ᎣᏏᏳ ᎿᎭᏛᏁᎭ.  ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᎾᏫᎭ.
200219.phonetic: Ni-hi ho-hi-yu-hv-s-ga a-si-yv-wi e-hv U-ne-la-nv-hi.  O-si-yu hna-dv-ne-ha.  A-ni-s-gi-na na-s-quo a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-ga, a-le du-ni-na-wi-ha.

200220.english: But wilt thou know, O vain man, that faith without works is dead?
200220.cherokee: ᏣᏚᎵᏍᎪᏃ ᏣᎦᏙᎥᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᏂᏣᏓᏅᏛᎾ ᎯᏍᎦᏯ, ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎵᏬᏨᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎵ ᏄᏛᏛᎾ ᏱᎩ?
200220.phonetic: Tsa-du-li-s-go-no tsa-ga-do-v-hi-s-di-yi, ni-tsa-da-nv-dv-na hi-s-ga-ya, go-hi-yu-di ge-sv u-li-wo-tsv-hi ge-sv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv ta-li nu-dv-dv-na yi-gi?

200221.english: Was not Abraham our father justified by works, when he had offered Isaac his son upon the altar?
200221.cherokee: ᏝᏍᎪ ᎡᏆᎭᎻ ᎢᎩᏙᏓ ᏳᏚᏓᎴᏍᏔᏁ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ, ᎤᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎡᏏᎩ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎨᎳᏍᏗᏱ?
200221.phonetic: Tla-s-go E-qua-ha-mi i-gi-do-da yu-du-da-le-s-ta-ne du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i, u-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv u-we-tsi E-si-gi a-tsi-lv ge-la-s-di-yi?

200222.english: Seest thou how faith wrought with his works, and by works was faith made perfect?
200222.cherokee: ᎯᎪᏩᏘᎭ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏍᏕᎸᎲ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᎤᏩᏔᏅ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎧᎵᎸᎢ.
200222.phonetic: Hi-go-wa-ti-ha go-hi-yu-di ge-sv u-s-de-lv-hv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i, na-s-gi-no du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv u-wa-ta-nv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv u-ka-li-lv-i.

200223.english: And the scripture was fulfilled which saith, Abraham believed God, and it was imputed unto him for righteousness: and he was called the Friend of God.
200223.cherokee: ᎪᏪᎸᏃ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏁᎢ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᎭ; "ᎡᏆᎭᎻ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏅᎩ ᎤᏁᎸᏅᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏠᏱ ᎠᏥᏰᎸᎾᏁᎸᎩ."  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎾᎵᎢ ᎠᎪᏎᎲᎩ.
200223.phonetic: Go-we-lv-no u-do-hi-yu-ne-i hi-a tsi-ni-ga-we-ha; "E-qua-ha-mi u-wo-hi-yu-nv-gi U-ne-lv-nv-hi, na-s-gi-no du-yu-go-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv u-tlo-yi a-tsi-ye-lv-na-ne-lv-gi."  Na-s-gi-no U-ne-la-nv-hi U-na-li-i a-go-se-hv-gi.

200224.english: Ye see then how that by works a man is justified, and not by faith only.
200224.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ ᏴᏫ ᏕᎤᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎠᏚᏓᎴᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᎪᎯᏳᏗᏉ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏩᏒ.
200224.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-tsi-go-wa-ti-ha yv-wi de-u-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv a-du-da-le-s-di-s-gv-i, v-tla-no go-hi-yu-di-quo ge-sv u-wa-sv.

200225.english: Likewise also was not Rahab the harlot justified by works, when she had received the messengers, and had sent them out another way?
200225.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎴᎭᏫ ᎤᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᏝᏍᎪ ᏕᎤᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᏳᏚᏓᎴᏍᏔᏁᎢ ᏚᏓᏂᎸᏨ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏅᏓᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏅᏫᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᏣᏘᏂ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᏂᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ?
200225.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo Le-ha-wi u-ne-gu-tsi-dv tla-s-go de-u-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv yu-du-da-le-s-ta-ne-i du-da-ni-lv-tsv na-s-gi na nv-da-ge-tsi-nv-si-dv a-le du-nv-wi-s-ta-nv u-tsa-ti-ni i-di-tlv wu-ni-lo-hi-s-di-yi?

200226.english: For as the body without the spirit is dead, so faith without works is dead also.
200226.cherokee: ᎠᏰᎸᏰᏃ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏄᏪᎲᎾ ᏱᎩ ᎤᏓᏬᏨᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎵ ᏄᏛᏛᎾ ᏱᎩ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᎵᏬᏨᎯ ᎨᏐᎢ.
200226.phonetic: A-ye-lv-ye-no a-da-nv-do nu-we-hv-na yi-gi u-da-wo-tsv-hi tsi-ge-so-i, na-s-gi-ya-quo go-hi-yu-di ge-sv ta-li nu-dv-dv-na yi-gi di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv na-s-quo u-li-wo-tsv-hi ge-so-i.

200301.english: My brethren, be not many masters, knowing that we shall receive the greater condemnation.
200301.cherokee: ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏣᏘ ᏗᏣᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ ᏱᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭᏰᏃ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎢᎩᏍᏛᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
200301.phonetic: I-da-li-nv-tli tle-s-di i-tsi-tsa-ti di-tsa-de-yo-hv-s-gi yi-ge-se-s-di, i-tsi-ga-ta-ha-ye-no u-tli i-ga-i i-gi-s-dv-di-s-di ge-sv-i;

200302.english: For in many things we offend all. If any man offend not in word, the same is a perfect man, and able also to bridle the whole body.
200302.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏘᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᎩ ᏂᏗᎥ ᎢᏗᏍᎦᏅᎪᎢ.  ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎬᎾ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎪᎸᎾ ᎨᏐ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᏰᎵᏉ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏪᎾᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎬ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ.
200302.phonetic: U-tsa-ti-ye-no i-yu-da-le-gi ni-di-v i-di-s-ga-nv-go-i.  I-yu-no gi-lo ga-wo-ni-s-gv na-s-ga-nv-gv-na tsi-ge-so-i, na-s-gi nu-go-lv-na ge-so a-s-ga-ya, ye-li-quo na-s-quo gv-we-na-hi-s-do-di ni-gv a-ye-lv-i.

200303.english: Behold, we put bits in the horses' mouths, that they may obey us; and we turn about their whole body.
200303.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏕᏗᎭᎾᎳᏗᏍᎪ ᏐᏈᎵ ᎨᎪᎯᏳᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎬ ᏗᏂᏰᎸ ᏕᏗᎪᎸᏏᏙᎰᎢ.
200303.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo de-di-ha-na-la-di-s-go so-qui-li ge-go-hi-yu-di-yi, a-le ni-gv di-ni-ye-lv de-di-go-lv-si-do-ho-i.

200304.english: Behold also the ships, which though they be so great, and are driven of fierce winds, yet are they turned about with a very small helm, whithersoever the governor listeth.
200304.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᏳᏧᏛᎾ ᏤᏉᎯᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᎦᏎᏗ ᎤᏃᎴ ᏕᎦᏂᏙᎰᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᏓᏂᎦᏔᎲᏏᏙᎰ ᎠᏅᏘᏍᎪ ᎤᏍᏗᎩᏳ ᎦᏌᏛᏍᏗ ᎾᎿᎭᏉ ᎢᏗᏢ ᎤᏚᎸ ᎦᏌᏛᏍᎩ.
200304.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo na-s-quo tsi-yu-tsu-dv-na tse-quo-hi-yu-s-gi-ni-no-nv a-le u-s-ga-se-di u-no-le de-ga-ni-do-ho-i, a-se-no da-ni-ga-ta-hv-si-do-ho a-nv-ti-s-go u-s-di-gi-yu ga-sa-dv-s-di na-hna-quo i-di-tlv u-du-lv ga-sa-dv-s-gi.

200305.english: Even so the tongue is a little member, and boasteth great things. Behold, how great a matter a little fire kindleth!
200305.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᏃᎦ ᎤᏍᏗᎩᏳ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎡᏉᎯᏳ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎪᎢ.  ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᎸ ᏄᏣᏔ ᎠᏓ ᏓᏥᏍᏢᏍᎪᎢ.
200305.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo ga-no-ga u-s-di-gi-yu, a-se-no e-quo-hi-yu ga-wo-ni-s-go-i.  Gv-ni-yu-quo u-s-di a-tsi-lv nu-tsa-ta a-da da-tsi-s-tlv-s-go-i.

200306.english: And the tongue is a fire, a world of iniquity: so is the tongue among our members, that it defileth the whole body, and setteth on fire the course of nature; and it is set on fire of hell.
200306.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎦᏃᎦ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᎧᎵᎢ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏄᏍᏗ ᎦᏃᎦ ᎤᏓᏑᏴ ᎢᎦᏙᏢᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎢᎬᏁᎯ ᏂᎬ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᏍᏢᏍᎩ ᎠᎢᏒ ᎠᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏨᏍᎩᏃᎢ ᎤᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ ᎠᏥᏥᏍᏝᏫᏍᏗᏍᎩ.
200306.phonetic: A-le ga-no-ga a-tsi-lv na-s-gi-ya-i a-le e-lo-hi a-ka-li-i ni-du-yu-go-dv-na ge-sv na-s-gi-ya.  Na-s-gi-ya nu-s-di ga-no-ga u-da-su-yv i-ga-do-tlv-sv-i, na-s-gi ga-da-ha i-gv-ne-hi ni-gv a-ye-lv-i, a-le a-tsi-s-tlv-s-gi a-i-sv a-ne-la-nv-hi ge-sv-i, a-le tsv-s-gi-no-i u-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi a-tsi-tsi-s-tla-wi-s-di-s-gi.

200307.english: For every kind of beasts, and of birds, and of serpents, and of things in the sea, is tamed, and hath been tamed of mankind:
200307.cherokee: ᏄᎾᏓᎴᏒᏰᏃ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏃᎯᎵᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏓᎾᏏᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᏗᏓᏅᏘᏐᏙᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏓᏅᏘᏐᏔᏅᎯ ᏴᏫ.
200307.phonetic: Nu-na-da-le-sv-ye-no i-na-ge a-ne-hi a-le a-ni-no-hi-li-do-hi a-le a-na-da-na-si-ni-do-hi a-le a-me-quo-hi a-ne-hi, di-da-nv-ti-so-do-di a-le tsu-da-nv-ti-so-ta-nv-hi yv-wi.

200308.english: But the tongue can no man tame; it is an unruly evil, full of deadly poison.
200308.cherokee: ᎦᏃᎦᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏰᎵ ᏴᎬᏓᏅᏘᏐᏓ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏲᎢ ᎬᏎᎪᎩᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎧᎵᎢ ᎠᏓᎯᎯ ᏅᏬᏘ.
200308.phonetic: Ga-no-ga-s-gi-ni v-tla gi-lo ye-li yv-gv-da-nv-ti-so-da, na-s-gi u-yo-i gv-se-go-gi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na, a-ka-li-i a-da-hi-hi nv-wo-ti.

200309.english: Therewith bless we God, even the Father; and therewith curse we men, which are made after the similitude of God.
200309.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏛᏗᏍᎪᎢ ᎡᏗᎸᏉᏗᏍᎪ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏛᏗᏍᎪᎢ ᏕᏗᏍᎩᏅᏗᏍᎪ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᎪᏢᏅᎯ
200309.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-dv-di-s-go-i e-di-lv-quo-di-s-go U-ne-la-nv-hi A-ga-yv-li-ge-i, a-le na-s-gi i-dv-di-s-go-i de-di-s-gi-nv-di-s-go yv-wi na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi nu-s-dv i-ge-gv-ne-lv-hi ge-go-tlv-nv-hi

200310.english: Out of the same mouth proceedeth blessing and cursing. My brethren, these things ought not so to be.
200310.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏌᏉ ᎠᎰᎵ ᏗᎦᏄᎪᎪ ᎦᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᎩᏅᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᎥᏝ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏄᏍᏗ.
200310.phonetic: Na-s-gi sa-quo a-ho-li di-ga-nu-go-go ga-lv-quo-do-di ge-sv a-le a-s-gi-nv-di-s-di ge-sv-i.  I-da-li-nv-tli, v-tla o-si-yu yi-gi na-s-gi tsi-nu-s-di.

200311.english: Doth a fountain send forth at the same place sweet water and bitter?
200311.cherokee: ᏥᎪ ᏌᏉ ᎦᏄᎪᎬ ᏗᎦᏄᎪᎪ ᎤᎦᎾᏍᏛ ᎠᎹ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏴᏍᏗ?
200311.phonetic: Tsi-go sa-quo ga-nu-go-gv di-ga-nu-go-go u-ga-na-s-dv a-ma a-le u-yv-s-di?

200312.english: Can the fig tree, my brethren, bear olive berries? either a vine, figs? so can no fountain both yield salt water and fresh.
200312.cherokee: ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᏥᎪ ᏒᎦᏔ-ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏡᎬ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏯᎾᏓᏛᎦ ᎣᎵᏩ ᏡᎬ ᎠᎾᏓᏛᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᎪ ᏖᎸᎳᏗᎯ ᏒᎦᎳ-ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏯᎾᏓᏛᎦ?  ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᏝ ᏰᎵ ᏌᏉ ᎦᏄᎪᎬ ᏴᏛᎦᏄᎪᎢ ~ᎠᎹ ᎤᎶᏥᏛ ᎠᎴ ~ᎠᎹ ᎤᎶᏥᏛ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
200312.phonetic: I-da-li-nv-tli, tsi-go sv-ga-ta--i-yu-s-di tlu-gv ye-li-quo ya-na-da-dv-ga o-li-wa tlu-gv a-na-da-dv-s-gi, a-le tsi-go te-lv-la-di-hi sv-ga-la--i-yu-s-di ya-na-da-dv-ga?  Na-s-quo-s-gi-ni v-tla ye-li sa-quo ga-nu-go-gv yv-dv-ga-nu-go-i ~a-ma u-lo-tsi-dv a-le ~a-ma u-lo-tsi-dv ni-ge-sv-na.

200313.english: Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom.
200313.cherokee: ᎦᎪ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏣᏛᏅᎢ ᎤᏓᏑᏯ ᎠᏏᎾᏌᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏔᏂᎢ?  ᎾᏍᎩ ᏫᎦᎾᏄᎪᏩ ᏕᎤᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ ᏫᎬᏙᏓ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎾᏛᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᎬᏙᏓ ᎠᏏᎾᏌᏂᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏔᎵ ᎤᏛᏕᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200313.phonetic: Ga-go ni-hi ni-tsa-dv-nv-i u-da-su-ya a-si-na-sa-ni a-le a-ga-ta-ni-i?  Na-s-gi wi-ga-na-nu-go-wa de-u-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i wi-gv-do-da o-si-yu na-dv-ne-hv-i, a-le wi-gv-do-da a-si-na-sa-ni-yu ge-sv ta-li u-dv-de-s-di u-da-nv-ti ge-sv-i.

200314.english: But if ye have bitter envying and strife in your hearts, glory not, and lie not against the truth.
200314.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᏕᏍᏗ ᏙᏗᏣᏓᏅᏛᎢ ᎠᏛᏳᎨᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏗᏒᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏣᏢᏆᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏣᏤᎪᎥᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏒ ᏱᏥᏲᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
200314.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni i-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-de-s-di do-di-tsa-da-nv-dv-i a-dv-yu-ge-di a-le a-di-sv-s-di ge-sv-i, tle-s-di yi-tsa-tlv-qua-s-ge-s-di a-le tle-s-di yi-tsa-tse-go-v-s-ge-s-di u-do-hi-yu-sv yi-tsi-yo-s-ge-s-di.

200315.english: This wisdom descendeth not from above, but is earthly, sensual, devilish.
200315.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏏᎾᏌᏂᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎡᎶᎯᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏊᏓᎸᏉ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
200315.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi a-si-na-sa-ni-yu ge-sv v-tla ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi yi-gi, e-lo-hi-quo-s-gi-ni e-hi a-le u-quu-da-lv-quo e-hi a-le a-s-gi-na i-yu-s-di.

200316.english: For where envying and strife is, there is confusion and every evil work.
200316.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏰᏃ ᎠᏛᏳᎨᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏗᏒᏍᏗ ᎠᏁᎲᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᎡᎰ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏲ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200316.phonetic: Na-hna-ye-no a-dv-yu-ge-di a-le a-di-sv-s-di a-ne-hv-i, na-hna e-ho tsu-da-le-nv-dv a-le ni-ga-v u-yo di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i.

200317.english: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and easy to be intreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.
200317.cherokee: ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎠᏏᎾᏌᏂᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏐᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎪᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏅᏘ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᎯᏍᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎧᎵᏬᎯ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏓᎸᏉᏛᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏠᎾᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
200317.phonetic: Ga-lv-la-di-s-gi-ni nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi tsi-gi a-si-na-sa-ni-yu ge-sv i-gv-yi-yi ga-da-ha ni-ge-sv-na ge-so-i, hna-quo-no go-hi-s-di-s-gi, a-le u-da-nv-ti, a-le u-wo-hi-yu-hi-s-dv, a-le a-ka-li-wo-hi a-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv-i a-le o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i, a-le nu-da-lv-quo-dv-na, a-le u-tlo-na-s-di ni-ge-sv-na.

200318.english: And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace of them that make peace.
200318.cherokee: ᎠᏃᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏃᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᏂᏫᏍᎪ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200318.phonetic: A-no-hi-s-di-s-gi-ni a-no-hi-s-di-s-gv-i a-ni-wi-s-go du-yu-go-dv u-na-nu-go-wi-s-di ge-sv-i.

200401.english: From whence come wars and fightings among you? come they not hence, even of your lusts that war in your members?
200401.cherokee: ᎭᏢ ᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ ᏓᎿᎭᏩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏤᎲᎴ?  ᏝᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏚᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏗᏥᏰᎸ ᎭᏫᏂ ᏥᏓᎵᎭ ᎾᎿᎭᏴᏗᏓᎴᎲᏍᎦ?
200401.phonetic: Ha-tlv di-da-le-hv-s-ga da-hna-wa a-le di-li-s-di ge-sv ni-hi i-tse-hv-le?  Tla-s-go na-s-gi a-du-lv-di ge-sv di-tsi-ye-lv ha-wi-ni tsi-da-li-ha na-hna yv-di-da-le-hv-s-ga?

200402.english: Ye lust, and have not: ye kill, and desire to have, and cannot obtain: ye fight and war, yet ye have not, because ye ask not.
200402.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏚᎵᎪᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᏩᏘᏍᎪᎢ; ᏕᏣᏓᎯᎰᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏤᎵᎯᏍᎪ ᎢᏥᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᏩᏘᏍᎪᎢ; ᏕᏣᎵᎰᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏓᎿᎭᏩ ᎢᏤᎪᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᏩᏘᏍᎪ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎪ ᏂᏥᏔᏲᎯᎲᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200402.phonetic: I-ga-du-li-go-i, a-se-no v-tla yi-tsi-wa-ti-s-go-i; de-tsa-da-hi-ho-i a-le i-tse-li-hi-s-go i-tsi-wa-dv-di-yi a-se-no v-tla yi-tsi-wa-ti-s-go-i; de-tsa-li-ho-i a-le da-hna-wa i-tse-go-i, a-se-no v-tla yi-tsi-wa-ti-s-go nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-go ni-tsi-ta-yo-hi-hv-na ge-sv-i.

200403.english: Ye ask, and receive not, because ye ask amiss, that ye may consume it upon your lusts.
200403.cherokee: ᎢᏥᏔᏲᎯᎰᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᏩᏘᏍᎪᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎪ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏥᏔᏲᎯᎲᎢ, ᎢᏣᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏨᏙᏗᏱ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎢᏥᏩᏛᏗᏱ.
200403.phonetic: I-tsi-ta-yo-hi-ho-i, a-se-no v-tla yi-tsi-wa-ti-s-go-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-go ni-du-yu-go-dv-na ge-sv i-tsi-ta-yo-hi-hv-i, i-tsa-du-li-s-gv na-s-gi i-tsv-do-di-yi o-s-dv i-tsa-da-nv-da-di-s-di-s-gi i-tsi-wa-dv-di-yi.

200404.english: Ye adulterers and adulteresses, know ye not that the friendship of the world is enmity with God? whosoever therefore will be a friend of the world is the enemy of God.
200404.cherokee: ᏗᏣᏓᏲᏁᎯ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᎨᏴ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᏱᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎾᎵᎢ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏍᎦᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ?  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎾᎵᎢ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏯᏓᏅᏖᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏍᎦᎩ ᎨᏐᎢ.
200404.phonetic: Di-tsa-da-yo-ne-hi i-tsi-s-ga-ya a-le i-tsi-ge-yv, tla-s-go yi-tsi-ga-ta-ha e-lo-hi u-na-li-i ge-sv-i U-ne-la-nv-hi a-s-ga-gi ge-sv-i?  Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di i-yu-no gi-lo e-lo-hi u-na-li-i i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi ya-da-nv-te-ha na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-s-ga-gi ge-so-i.

200405.english: Do ye think that the scripture saith in vain, The spirit that dwelleth in us lusteth to envy?
200405.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏉᏍᎪ ᏂᎦᏪ ᎢᏥᏰᎸᎭ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎦᏪ ᎪᏪᎵ; ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏥᎩᏯᎠ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎠᏛᏳᎨᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏩᎦᏘ.
200405.phonetic: A-se-quo-s-go ni-ga-we i-tsi-ye-lv-ha hi-a tsi-ni-ga-we go-we-li; A-da-nv-do tsi-gi-ya-a u-du-li-s-gv a-dv-yu-ge-di ge-sv wa-ga-ti.

200406.english: But he giveth more grace. Wherefore he saith, God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.
200406.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏟ ᎢᎦᎢ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏓᏁᎰᎢ-ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᎭ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎾᏢᏉᏗ ᏓᏡᏗᏍᎪᎢ, ᎤᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎩᏂ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏓᏁᎰᎢ.
200406.phonetic: A-se-no u-tli i-ga-i gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv da-ne-ho-i---Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di hi-a ni-ga-we-ha; U-ne-la-nv-hi u-na-tlv-quo-di da-tlu-di-s-go-i, u-na-da-do-li-s-di-s-gi-ni gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv da-ne-ho-i.

200407.english: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
200407.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏤᏣᎴᏲᎯᏏ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ; ᏤᏣᏟᏴᏏ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏎ ᏓᏣᏓᏅᎡᎵ.
200407.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tse-tsa-le-yo-hi-si U-ne-la-nv-hi; Tse-tsa-tli-yv-si a-s-gi-na, a-le a-se da-tsa-da-nv-e-li.

200408.english: Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you. Cleanse your hands, ye sinners; and purify your hearts, ye double minded.
200408.cherokee: ᎾᎥ ᎡᏥᎷᏥᏏ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎾᎥ ᏓᏥᎷᏤᎵ.  ᏗᏦᏑᎵ ᏗᏦᏰᏂ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᎾᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏥᏅᎦᎸ ᏗᏥᎾᏫ ᏔᎵ ᎢᏧᏓᎢ ᏗᏣᏓᏅᏘ.
200408.phonetic: Na-v e-tsi-lu-tsi-si U-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi-no ni-hi na-v da-tsi-lu-tse-li.  Di-tso-su-li di-tso-ye-ni i-tsi-s-ga-na-i, a-le di-tsi-nv-ga-lv di-tsi-na-wi ta-li i-tsu-da-i di-tsa-da-nv-ti.

200409.english: Be afflicted, and mourn, and weep: let your laughter be turned to mourning, and your joy to heaviness.
200409.cherokee: ᎤᏲ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏓᏓ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏥᏴᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏣᏠᏱᎦ; ᎢᏥᏰᏣᏍᎬᎢ ᎠᏴᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏩᏓᏁᏟᏴᏍᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᎵᎮᎵᎬ ᎤᏲ ᎠᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏩᏓᏁᏟᏴᏍᏓ.
200409.phonetic: U-yo i-tsa-da-nv-da-da a-le di-tsi-yv-la a-le di-tsa-tlo-yi-ga; i-tsi-ye-tsa-s-gv-i a-yv-li-s-di ge-sv wa-da-ne-tli-yv-s-da, a-le i-tsa-li-he-li-gv u-yo a-da-nv-da-di-s-di ge-sv wa-da-ne-tli-yv-s-da.

200410.english: Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he shall lift you up.
200410.cherokee: ᎢᏣᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏱᎰᏩ ᏙᏗᎧᏂᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏓᏥᏌᎳᏓᏂ.
200410.phonetic: I-tsa-da-do-li-s-di ge-se-s-di Yi-ho-wa do-di-ka-ni-s-gv-i, na-s-gi-no da-tsi-sa-la-da-ni.

200411.english: Speak not evil one of another, brethren. He that speaketh evil of his brother, and judgeth his brother, speaketh evil of the law, and judgeth the law: but if thou judge the law, thou art not a doer of the law, but a judge.
200411.cherokee: ᏞᏍᏗ ᎤᏐᏅ ᏱᏗᏣᏓᏃᎮᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ; ᎤᏐᏅ ᏥᎧᏃᎮᏍᎪ ᏥᎧᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏕᎫᎪᏓᏁᎰ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏐᏅ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎪ ᎧᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎪ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏕᎫᎪᏓᏁᎰ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏱᏚᎪᏓᏁᎭ ᎥᏝ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏘᎧᎿᎭᏩᏕᎩ ᏱᎦᎩ ᏚᎪᏓᏁᎯᏍᎩᏂ.
200411.phonetic: Tle-s-di u-so-nv yi-di-tsa-da-no-he-s-ge-s-di i-da-da-nv-tli; u-so-nv tsi-ka-no-he-s-go tsi-ka-ne-i-s-di-s-go di-na-da-nv-tli a-le tsi-de-gu-go-da-ne-ho di-na-da-nv-tli, Na-s-gi u-so-nv ka-no-he-s-go ka-ne-i-s-di-s-go di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di a-le de-gu-go-da-ne-ho di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di; i-yu-no di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di yi-du-go-da-ne-ha v-tla di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di ti-ka-hna-wa-de-gi yi-ga-gi du-go-da-ne-hi-s-gi-ni.

200412.english: There is one lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy: who art thou that judgest another?
200412.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎡᎭ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎪᏢᏍᎩ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎬᏩᏓᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏓᎯᏍᏗ; ᎦᎪ ᏂᎯ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏥᏕᎯᏳᎪᏓᏁᎭ?
200412.phonetic: A-si-yv-wi e-ha di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di go-tlv-s-gi ye-li-quo gv-wa-da-s-de-lv-di a-le gv-wa-da-hi-s-di; ga-go ni-hi nv-wa-da-le tsi-de-hi-yu-go-da-ne-ha?

200413.english: Go to now, ye that say, To day or to morrow we will go into such a city, and continue there a year, and buy and sell, and get gain:
200413.cherokee: Ꭷ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏥᏪᏍᎩ; ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏑᎾᎴᎢ ᏓᏲᏤᏏ ᎨᎯ ᏗᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᏑᏕᏘᏴᏛᏃ ᏬᏤᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏬᏥᏃᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏬᎩᏁᏉᏤᎮᏍᏗ;
200413.phonetic: Ka hi-a i-tsi-we-s-gi; Go-hi i-ga a-le su-na-le-i da-yo-tse-si ge-hi di-ga-du-hv-i, su-de-ti-yv-dv-no wo-tse-he-s-di a-le wo-tsi-no-di-s-ge-s-di a-le wo-gi-ne-quo-tse-he-s-di;

200414.english: Whereas ye know not what shall be on the morrow. For what is your life? It is even a vapour, that appeareth for a little time, and then vanisheth away.
200414.cherokee: ᏂᏥᎦᏔᎲᎾᏃ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏑᎾᎴᎢ; ᏕᏨᏅᏰᏃ ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ?  ᎤᎬᎭᏛᏉ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏞᎦ ᏥᎦᎾᏄᎪᎪᎢ ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏣᎵᏛᏗᏍᎪᎢ.
200414.phonetic: Ni-tsi-ga-ta-hv-na-no i-yu-li-s-do-di ge-sv su-na-le-i; De-tsv-nv-ye-no ga-do u-s-di?  U-gv-ha-dv-quo na-s-gi-ya-i na-s-gi tle-ga tsi-ga-na-nu-go-go-i hna-quo-no tsa-li-dv-di-s-go-i.

200415.english: For that ye ought to say, If the Lord will, we shall live, and do this, or that.
200415.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏳᎬᏫᏳᎭ ᏱᏂᏥᏪᎭ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎣᏏ ᏳᏰᎸᏅ, ᏙᏨᏁᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏃᏣᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠᏉ.
200415.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di hi-a Yu-gv-wi-yu-ha yi-ni-tsi-we-ha; I-yu-no U-gv-wi-yu-hi o-si yu-ye-lv-nv, do-tsv-ne-s-di a-le hi-a no-tsa-dv-ne-he-s-di a-le hi-a-quo.

200416.english: But now ye rejoice in your boastings: all such rejoicing is evil.
200416.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏣᎵᎮᎵᎦ ᎢᏣᏢᏆᏍᎬᎢ; ᏂᎦᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏲᏉ ᎨᏐᎢ.
200416.phonetic: Go-hi-s-gi-ni ge-sv i-tsa-li-he-li-ga i-tsa-tlv-qua-s-gv-i; ni-ga-dv na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-li-he-li-s-di ge-sv u-yo-quo ge-so-i.

200417.english: Therefore to him that knoweth to do good, and doeth it not, to him it is sin.
200417.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᏣᎦᏔᎰᎢ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᏛᏁᎲᎾ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎿ ᎭᎠᏍᎦᏅᎪᎢ.
200417.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di gi-lo tsa-ga-ta-ho-i o-s-dv i-ya-dv-ne-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi-no na-dv-ne-hv-na tsi-ge-so-i, na-s-gi na-hna a-s-ga-nv-go-i.

200501.english: Go to now, ye rich men, weep and howl for your miseries that shall come upon you.
200501.cherokee: Ꭷ ᏂᎯ ᏗᏤᎿᎭᎢ ᏗᏣᏠᏱᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏥᏴᎳ ᏫᏂᎦᎵᏍᏙᏓ ᎢᏥᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᎷᏤᏗ ᏥᎩ.
200501.phonetic: Ka ni-hi di-tse-hna-i di-tsa-tlo-yi-ga a-le di-tsi-yv-la wi-ni-ga-li-s-do-da i-tsi-gi-li-yo-i-s-di i-tsi-lu-tse-di tsi-gi.

200502.english: Your riches are corrupted, and your garments are motheaten.
200502.cherokee: ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎢᏥᎲᎢ ᎤᎪᏒ; ᏗᏣᏄᏬᏃ ᏥᏍᎪᏴ ᎦᏂᏯᎥ;
200502.phonetic: Tsu-gv-wa-lo-di i-tsi-hv-i u-go-sv; di-tsa-nu-wo-no tsi-s-go-yv ga-ni-ya-v;

200503.english: Your gold and silver is cankered; and the rust of them shall be a witness against you, and shall eat your flesh as it were fire. Ye have heaped treasure together for the last days.
200503.cherokee: ᏗᏣᏤᎵ ᏓᎶᏂᎨ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎬ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏚᏯᏍᏜᏓᏛ, ᏚᏯᏍᏜᏓᏛᏃ ᎢᏥᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏰᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏇᏓᎸ ᎠᏥᎸ ᏥᎦᏰᏍᎪ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ.  ᎢᏥᏟᏌᏅ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᎸᏗ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
200503.phonetic: Di-tsa-tse-li da-lo-ni-ge a-de-lv a-le u-ne-gv a-de-lv du-ya-s-dla-dv, du-ya-s-dla-dv-no i-tsi-no-he-s-gi ge-se-s-di, a-le u-we-ye-s-di ge-se-s-di i-tsi-que-da-lv a-tsi-lv tsi-ga-ye-s-go na-s-gi-ya-i.  I-tsi-tli-sa-nv tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-li-s-qua-lv-di i-ga ge-sv u-gv-wa-li.

200504.english: Behold, the hire of the labourers who have reaped down your fields, which is of you kept back by fraud, crieth: and the cries of them which have reaped are entered into the ears of the Lord of sabaoth.
200504.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏠᏱᎭ ᎨᎦᎫᏴᏓᏁᏗ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᎤᏂᏍᎫᏕᏒᎯ ᏕᏥᎶᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏥᎾᎯᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏛᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏍᎫᏕᏒᎯ ᏚᎵᎠᏴᎸ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏓᎿᎭᏩ ᎠᏁᎩ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
200504.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo a-tlo-yi-ha ge-ga-gu-yv-da-ne-di tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi u-ni-s-gu-de-sv-hi de-tsi-lo-ge-sv-i, na-s-gi di-tsi-na-hi-s-da-ne-lv-hi tsi-gi, u-na-li-s-dv-no na-s-gi u-ni-s-gu-de-sv-hi du-li-a-yv-lv U-gv-wi-yu-hi da-hna-wa a-ne-gi u-na-tse-li-ga.

200505.english: Ye have lived in pleasure on the earth, and been wanton; ye have nourished your hearts, as in a day of slaughter.
200505.cherokee: ᎤᏢᏉᏗ ᎢᏤᎲᎩ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏂᏣᏁᎸᎾ; ᏕᏤᎶᎸ ᏗᏥᎾᏫ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏥᎾᎾᏛᏁᎰ ᏗᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒ ᎢᎦ ᎠᏍᏆᎸ.
200505.phonetic: U-tlv-quo-di i-tse-hv-gi e-lo-hi a-le ni-tsa-ne-lv-na; de-tse-lo-lv di-tsi-na-wi, na-s-gi-ya tsi-na-na-dv-ne-ho di-hi-s-di-yi ge-sv i-ga a-s-qua-lv.

200506.english: Ye have condemned and killed the just; and he doth not resist you.
200506.cherokee: ᏕᎫᎪᏓᏁᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎡᏥᎸ ᎾᏍᎩᏅᎾ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᏱᏣᏞᏤᎭ.
200506.phonetic: De-gu-go-da-ne-lv a-le e-tsi-lv na-s-gi-nv-na, v-tla-no yi-tsa-tle-tse-ha.

200507.english: Be patient therefore, brethren, unto the coming of the Lord. Behold, the husbandman waiteth for the precious fruit of the earth, and hath long patience for it, until he receive the early and latter rain.
200507.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ ᏗᏨᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎬᏂ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎦᎷᏨᎭ.  ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᎶᎨᏛ ᎠᎦᏘᏲ ᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏐ ᎠᎦᏘᏲᎢ, ᎬᏂ ᎤᎦᎾᏏ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏂᏱ ᎤᎦᏃᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200507.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-da-li-nv-tli di-tsv-ni-di-yu ge-se-s-di gv-ni U-gv-wi-yu-hi ga-lu-tsv-ha.  Gv-ni-yu-quo u-lo-ge-dv a-ga-ti-yo ga-lv-quo-di e-lo-hi u-na-nu-go-wi-sv-hi, a-le gv-ni-di-yu ge-so a-ga-ti-yo-i, gv-ni u-ga-na-si i-gv-yi-yi a-le o-ni-yi u-ga-no-di ge-sv-i.

200508.english: Be ye also patient; stablish your hearts: for the coming of the Lord draweth nigh.
200508.cherokee: ᏂᎯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏗᏨᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᏗᏣᎵᏂᎪᎯᏍᏓ ᏗᏥᎾᏫ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏰᏃ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᎯᏗ.
200508.phonetic: Ni-hi na-s-quo di-tsv-ni-di-yu ge-se-s-di; di-tsa-li-ni-go-hi-s-da di-tsi-na-wi, U-gv-wi-yu-hi-ye-no u-lu-hi-s-di-yi u-s-qua-lv-hi-di.

200509.english: Grudge not one against another, brethren, lest ye be condemned: behold, the judge standeth before the door.
200509.cherokee: ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏗᏣᏓᏍᎦᎨᏍᏗ ᏱᏕᏧᎪᏓᏏᏰᏃ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ.  ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏗᎫᎪᏗᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᏙᎦ.
200509.phonetic: I-da-da-nv-tli tle-s-di yi-di-tsa-da-s-ga-ge-s-di yi-de-tsu-go-da-si-ye-no i-tsi-s-ga-nv-tsv-i.  Gv-ni-yu-quo Di-gu-go-di-s-gi ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi ga-do-ga.

200510.english: Take, my brethren, the prophets, who have spoken in the name of the Lord, for an example of suffering affliction, and of patience.
200510.cherokee: ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏙᏗ ᎠᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏕᎤᏙᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᏁᎢᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᏗᏥᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
200510.phonetic: I-da-da-nv-tli a-do-le-ho-hi-s-do-di a-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-sv a-le gv-ni-di-yu ge-sv-i, a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi de-u-do-s-dv u-ni-ne-i-s-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi, di-tsi-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di ge-se-s-di.

200511.english: Behold, we count them happy which endure. Ye have heard of the patience of Job, and have seen the end of the Lord; that the Lord is very pitiful, and of tender mercy.
200511.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎢᎩᏰᎸᏐ ᏗᏅᏂᏗᏳ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ.  ᎢᏣᏛᎦᏅ ᎬᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏦᏈ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᎪᎲ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏭᏰᎢᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏣᏘᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200511.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo o-si-yu i-yu-na-li-s-da-ne-di i-gi-ye-lv-so di-nv-ni-di-yu tsi-ge-so-i.  I-tsa-dv-ga-nv gv-ni-di-yu ge-sv Tso-qui, a-le i-tsi-go-hv nu-s-dv wu-ye-i-lo-hi-s-di ge-sv U-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-le na-s-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tsa-ti u-da-nv-ti-yu ge-sv a-le u-da-do-li-tsa-ti-yu ge-sv-i.

200512.english: But above all things, my brethren, swear not, neither by heaven, neither by the earth, neither by any other oath: but let your yea be yea; and your nay, nay; lest ye fall into condemnation.
200512.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᏭᏓᎪᎾᏛᏛᎢ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏣᏎᎵᏛᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏱᏥᏁᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎦᎸᎶᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏄᏓᎴᏒᏉ ᎠᏎᎵᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏄᏓᎴᏒᏉ ᎠᏎᎵᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎢᏣᏤᎵᏍᎩᏂ ᎥᎥ ᎢᏣᏛᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎥᎥ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏤᎵ ᎥᏝ ᎢᏣᏛᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏞᏍᏗ ᏤᏧᎪᏓᏁᎸᎩ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ.
200512.phonetic: A-se-no i-da-da-nv-tli, wu-da-go-na-dv-dv-i, tle-s-di yi-tsa-se-li-dv-s-ge-s-di yi-tsi-ne-i-s-di-s-ge-s-di ga-lv-lo-i, a-le e-lo-hi, a-le tle-s-di nu-da-le-sv-quo a-se-li-do-di ge-sv-i; a-le tle-s-di nu-da-le-sv-quo a-se-li-do-di ge-sv-i; i-tsa-tse-li-s-gi-ni v-v i-tsa-dv-di ge-sv v-v ge-se-s-di, a-le i-tsa-tse-li v-tla i-tsa-dv-di ge-sv v-tla ge-se-s-di; na-s-gi-no tle-s-di tse-tsu-go-da-ne-lv-gi i-tsi-s-ga-nv-tsv-i.

200513.english: Is any among you afflicted? let him pray. Is any merry? let him sing psalms.
200513.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎢᏤᎲ ᎤᏓᏑᏰᏍᏗ ᎠᎩᎵᏲᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎩᎶ ᎤᎦᎵᏍᏗ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᏍᏗ ᏕᎧᏃᎩᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
200513.phonetic: Gi-lo i-tse-hv u-da-su-ye-s-di a-gi-li-yo-ge-s-di, a-da-do-li-s-di-s-ge-s-di; Gi-lo u-ga-li-s-di u-da-nv-te-s-di de-ka-no-gi-s-ge-s-di.

200514.english: Is any sick among you? let him call for the elders of the church; and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord:
200514.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎢᏤᎲ ᎤᏓᏑᏰᏍᏗ ᎤᏢᎨᏍᏗ, ᏫᏓᏯᏂᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏗᎨᎦᏗᎶᏗ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏔᏲᎯᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ, ᎬᏩᎶᏁᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏠᏁᏗ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏚᏙᎥᎢ;
200514.phonetic: Gi-lo i-tse-hv u-da-su-ye-s-di u-tlv-ge-s-di, wi-da-ya-ni-s-ge-s-di di-ge-ga-di-lo-di tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv-i, na-s-gi-no a-na-da-do-li-s-di-s-ge-s-di a-ni-ta-yo-hi-he-s-di a-tsi-s-de-lv-di-yi, gv-wa-lo-ne-di-s-ge-s-di a-tlo-ne-di a-nv-di-s-ge-s-di U-gv-wi-yu-hi du-do-v-i;

200515.english: And the prayer of faith shall save the sick, and the Lord shall raise him up; and if he have committed sins, they shall be forgiven him.
200515.cherokee: ᎪᎯᏳᎯᏃ ᎠᏓᏃᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏓᏳᏍᏕᎸᎯ ᎤᏢᎩ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᏙᏓᏳᎴᏔᏂ; ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏚᏅᏨᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏥᏂᎯᏎᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
200515.phonetic: Go-hi-yu-hi-no a-da-no-li-s-do-di ge-sv da-yu-s-de-lv-hi u-tlv-gi, U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no do-da-yu-le-ta-ni; i-yu a-le u-s-du-nv-tsv-hi ge-se-s-di na-s-gi tsa-tsi-ni-hi-se-di ge-se-s-di.

200516.english: Confess your faults one to another, and pray one for another, that ye may be healed. The effectual fervent prayer of a righteous man availeth much.
200516.cherokee: ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏕᏣᏓᏛᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏅᏨᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᏣᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎮᏍᏗ, ᎢᏂᏔᏲᎯᎮᏍᏗ ᏗᏣᏗᏫᏍᏗᏱ.  ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎤᏰᎸᎯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏓᏅᏘ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᏓᏍᏕᎵᏍᎪᎢ.
200516.phonetic: Gv-ni-ge-sv ni-de-tsa-da-dv-ne-he-s-di i-tsi-s-ga-nv-tsv-di, a-le de-tsa-da-do-li-s-da-ne-he-s-di, i-ni-ta-yo-hi-he-s-di di-tsa-di-wi-s-di-yi.  U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya u-ye-lv-hi u-da-do-li-s-do-di ge-sv u-da-nv-ti u-tsa-ti a-da-s-de-li-s-go-i.

200517.english: Elias was a man subject to like passions as we are, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain: and it rained not on the earth by the space of three years and six months.
200517.cherokee: ᎢᎳᏯ ᏴᏫᏉ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎠᏴ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎤᏓᏅᏛᎩ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᎤᏔᏲᎸᎩ ᎤᎦᏃᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ; ᎰᏩᏃ ᏄᏚᎾᏅᎾ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏦᎢ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᏑᏓᎵᏃ ᎢᏯᏅᏙ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ.
200517.phonetic: I-la-ya yv-wi-quo ge-sv-gi a-yv ni-ga-li-s-da-ne-hv na-s-gi-ya i-gv-wa-li-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-gi; a-le u-li-ni-gi-dv u-da-nv-dv-gi u-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-gi u-ta-yo-lv-gi u-ga-no-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na; ho-wa-no nu-du-na-nv-na ge-sv-gi ga-do-hi tso-i tsu-de-ti-yv-dv su-da-li-no i-ya-nv-do i-go-hi-dv.

200518.english: And he prayed again, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth brought forth her fruit.
200518.cherokee: ᎠᎴᏬ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎦᎸᎶᎢᏃ ᎤᎦᏃᏓᏁᎸᎩ, ᎪᏙᎯᏃ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏒᎩ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
200518.phonetic: A-le-wo u-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-gi, ga-lv-lo-i-no u-ga-no-da-ne-lv-gi, go-do-hi-no u-na-nu-go-wi-sv-gi u-na-nu-go-wi-s-di ge-sv-i.

200519.english: Brethren, if any of you do err from the truth, and one convert him;
200519.cherokee: ᎢᏓᎵᏅᏟ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏣᏛᏅ ᎠᏞᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏚᏲᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᎤᎦᏔᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ,
200519.phonetic: I-da-li-nv-tli, i-yu-no gi-lo ni-hi ni-tsa-dv-nv a-tle-s-ge-s-di du-yo-s-ge-s-di du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i, gi-lo-no i-u-ga-ta-hv-s-ge-s-di,

200520.english: Let him know, that he which converteth the sinner from the error of his way shall save a soul from death, and shall hide a multitude of sins.
200520.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᎦᏔᎮᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᎾ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᏍᎩ ᎾᎿ ᎭᎤᎵᏓᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᎢᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬ ᎫᏓᎴᏍᎬ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᎩ ᎠᏍᏚᏅᏨᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏍᎦᎸᏍᎬᎢ.
200520.phonetic: Na-s-gi a-ga-ta-he-s-di, na-s-gi a-s-ga-na a-ga-ta-hv-s-gi na-hna u-li-da-s-ta-nv-hi a-i-sv-i, na-s-gi a-s-de-li-s-gv gu-da-le-s-gv a-da-nv-do a-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le u-tsa-ti i-yu-da-le-gi a-s-du-nv-tsv-hi ge-sv gv-s-ga-lv-s-gv-i.
